questions.question: What is a mineral?
questions.questionThai: แร่คืออะไร?
options: A natural solid substance with a specific chemical makeup
questions.explanation: A mineral is a natural solid substance found in Earth with a specific chemical makeup.

questions.question: What are rocks made of?
questions.questionThai: หินทำจากอะไร?
questions.explanation: Rocks are made of one or more minerals combined together.

questions.question: What is an igneous rock?
questions.questionThai: หินอัคนีคืออะไร?
options: Rock formed when melted rock cools and hardens
Rock changed by heat and pressure
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks form when hot, melted rock (magma or lava) cools down and becomes solid.

questions.question: What is a sedimentary rock?
questions.questionThai: หินตะกอนคืออะไร?
options: Rock formed from layers of sediment pressed together
questions.explanation: Sedimentary rocks form when layers of sand, mud, or shells get pressed together over millions of years.

questions.question: Where are fossils usually found?
questions.questionThai: ฟอสซิลมักจะพบที่ไหน?
questions.explanation: Fossils are usually found in sedimentary rocks because dead organisms get buried in sediment layers.

questions.question: What is lava?
questions.questionThai: ลาวาคืออะไร?
options: Hot melted rock that comes out of a volcano
questions.explanation: Lava is hot, melted rock that comes out of a volcano onto Earth's surface.

questions.question: What is granite?
questions.questionThai: หินแกรนิตคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: Granite is an igneous rock that formed when magma cooled slowly underground.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural mineral?
questions.questionThai: แร่ธรรมชาติที่แข็งที่สุดคืออะไร?
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest natural mineral on Earth.

questions.question: What is a metamorphic rock?
questions.questionThai: หินแปรคืออะไร?
options: Rock that has been changed by heat and pressure
questions.explanation: Metamorphic rocks are existing rocks that have been changed by extreme heat, pressure, or both.

questions.question: What is the rock cycle?
questions.questionThai: วัฏจักรหินคืออะไร?
options: The continuous process of rocks changing from one type to another
questions.explanation: The rock cycle is the continuous process where rocks change from one type to another over millions of years.

questions.question: Marble is a metamorphic rock that was originally:
questions.questionThai: หินอ่อนเป็นหินแปรที่เดิมทีเป็น:
questions.explanation: Marble forms when limestone is subjected to heat and pressure, changing its structure.

questions.question: What is erosion?
questions.questionThai: การกัดเซาะคืออะไร?
options: Rock being worn away by water, wind, or ice
questions.explanation: Erosion is the process of rock being worn away by water, wind, ice, or other forces.

questions.question: In the reading passage, what type of rock made up most of the cave?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน หินประเภทใดเป็นส่วนใหญ่ของถ้ำ?
questions.explanation: The cave was inside a limestone mountain. Limestone is a sedimentary rock.

questions.question: How do stalactites form in caves?
questions.questionThai: หินย้อยก่อตัวขึ้นในถ้ำอย่างไร?
options: They grow from the floor up
Water dripping from the ceiling deposits minerals over thousands of years
They are placed there by people
questions.explanation: Stalactites form when water dripping from cave ceilings deposits tiny bits of dissolved minerals over thousands of years.

questions.question: What property is used to describe how shiny a mineral is?
questions.questionThai: คุณสมบัติใดใช้อธิบายว่าแร่วาวแค่ไหน?
questions.explanation: Luster describes how a mineral's surface reflects light - whether it's metallic, glassy, or dull.

questions.question: What happens to magma when it reaches Earth's surface?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นกับแมกมาเมื่อมันขึ้นมาถึงผิวโลก?
questions.explanation: When magma reaches Earth's surface through a volcano, it is called lava.

questions.question: Why does granite have larger crystals than basalt?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมหินแกรนิตถึงมีผลึกใหญ่กว่าหินบะซอลต์?
options: Granite cooled slowly underground, giving crystals time to grow; basalt cooled quickly
They have the same crystal size
questions.explanation: Granite cooled slowly underground, allowing large crystals to form. Basalt cooled quickly on the surface, creating tiny crystals.

questions.question: In the reading passage, how did all three rock types form in the same cave?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน หินทั้งสามประเภทก่อตัวขึ้นในถ้ำเดียวกันได้อย่างไร?
options: They were put there by people
Limestone formed the cave, magma created basalt, and heat from magma changed limestone into marble
They all formed at the same time
questions.explanation: Sedimentary limestone made up the cave, igneous basalt formed when magma pushed in, and metamorphic marble formed where heat from magma changed the limestone.

questions.question: Why can any type of rock become any other type of rock?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมหินประเภทใดก็สามารถกลายเป็นหินประเภทอื่นได้?
options: The rock cycle shows that with enough time and the right conditions, rocks continuously change forms
Rocks choose which type to become
questions.explanation: The rock cycle shows that over millions of years, any rock can be melted, eroded, or changed by heat and pressure into any other type.

questions.question: Why is understanding rocks important for humans?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมการเข้าใจหินถึงสำคัญสำหรับมนุษย์?
options: Rocks provide building materials, contain valuable minerals, and tell us about Earth's history
We only use rocks for jewelry
questions.explanation: Understanding rocks helps us find useful minerals, build structures, and learn about Earth's history through fossils and rock layers.
